title: Generating Module Instance using Verilator
body:
Hi, I'm curious if there is a way to use Verilator to not just lint or compile a Verilog file, but also generate an instantiation. For large modules creating instantiation is a tedious and it would be good to just write for `something.v`

```
module something(input x, output y);
...
endmodule
```

the command:

```
verilator --dump-instantiation something.v
```

would magically print

```
something(  .x( _in_x  ),
            .y( _out_y ) );
```

or such, even including parameters, etc. Obviously this is more useful for large modules.
